title: gaura parviflora var. typica
intro: Gaura parviflora var. typica, also known as the Smallflower Beeblossom, is a perennial plant species native to the southeastern United States. It is known for its small white or pinkish flowers, which have medicinal properties, particularly as a diuretic and to treat fever. The plant is used to treat various health issues, including urinary tract infections, kidney stones, and inflammation, due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. Gaura parviflora var. typica can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusions to leverage its medicinal properties and health benefits.
